title: Entertainment Value of Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: Entertainment Value of Evangelion             Quote Post by Tumbling Down » Sat Oct 25, 2014 11:02 pm What is it you find entertaining about Evangelion? Not intellectually interesting, but entertaining .

[]:             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Sat Oct 25, 2014 11:54 pm Meh, entertainment value for NGE isn't very high, Rebuild on the other hand is chock full of entertainment. I mean unless you are entertained by angst and dysfunction, then NGE is right up your alley.

[]:             Quote Post by Sgt. Griff » Sat Oct 25, 2014 11:59 pm The action arc was pretty entertaining, Dance Like You Want To Win especially.

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Sun Oct 26, 2014 12:06 am The spectacle of seeing giant robot battles being done in a pseudo realistic way for one. The rest is the empathy we have with the characters, the slice of life comedy aspects. The thing is though Eva is meant to engage and inform its audience as much as it entertains, it baits you in with promises of a fun lighthearted Giant Robot anime. Then shows it's true colors as a grand tragedy about the pros, cons and inherent importance of human communication. Of course then we have to ask ourselves, what makes a work of fiction entertaining to begin with?

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sun Oct 26, 2014 1:06 am I find that intellectual interest and entertainment are very closely linked as far as I'm concerned. What did I find entertaining? Pretty much all of it. That's why I'm here on the forums, you know.

[]:             Quote Post by El Squibbonator » Sun Oct 26, 2014 4:35 pm I think Evangelion can still be considered entertainment. Sure, it has a lot of deep intellectual themes, but it's still fundamentally a TV series meant to provide entertainment.

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Sun Oct 26, 2014 4:43 pm El Squibbonator wrote: I think Evangelion can still be considered entertainment. Sure, it has a lot of deep intellectual themes, but it's still fundamentally a TV series meant to provide entertainment.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sun Oct 26, 2014 5:19 pm Entertainment is from the word “entertain”. Google wrote: en·ter·tain ˌen(t)ərˈtān/ verb 1. provide (someone) with amusement or enjoyment. "a tremendous game that thoroughly entertained the crowd" synonyms: amuse, divert, delight, please, charm, cheer, interest; More antonyms: bore receive (someone) as a guest and provide them with food and drink. "a private dining room where members could entertain groups of friends" synonyms: receive, host, play host/hostess to, invite (around/round/over), throw a party for; 2. give attention or consideration to (an idea, suggestion, or feeling). "Washington entertained little hope of an early improvement in relations" synonyms: consider, give consideration to, contemplate, think about, give thought to;

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Sun Oct 26, 2014 5:27 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: In the Next Preview at the end of Episode 17 the voice-over even invites the viewers to “bring their friends,” which would mean that Evangelion is defined in that Next Preview to be able to entertain a group of friends.

[]:             Quote Post by IronEvangelion » Mon Oct 27, 2014 12:10 am Let's see... A genetically engineered little girl trolling a female scientist, a teenage boy using his giant momborg to fight a giant living laser-tentacle dildo, an alchoholic major with a pet penguin trying to be a mother to a couple of teens who don't get along, Rei Ayanami, giant cyborgs fighting bizzarre-looking angels, and... just about everything else, too.

[]:             Quote Post by Compiling_Autumn » Mon Oct 27, 2014 12:13 am My answer is yes, except for episode 7 and maybe episode 13. The angel in episode 11 is pretty lame too

[]:             Quote Post by Rodeo » Mon Oct 27, 2014 2:48 pm Wouldn't be a very good show if it wasn't, Evangelion IS entertainment.

[]:             Quote Post by Tumbling Down » Mon Oct 27, 2014 7:11 pm I guess I'm in the minority. I find the action arc rather boring. The show doesn't really get good for me until the end.

[]:             Quote Post by mechashiva » Mon Oct 27, 2014 10:27 pm Sgt. Griff wrote: The action arc was pretty entertaining, Dance Like You Want To Win especially.

[]:             Quote Post by Giji Shinka » Tue Oct 28, 2014 6:22 am ChaddyManPrime wrote: Rebuild on the other hand is chock full of entertainment.

[]:             Quote Post by Rosenakahara » Tue Oct 28, 2014 9:18 am ^ and thats why i love NTE uh.........NTE or NGE? if NTE: the fights dear god are they awesome. if NGE:.....................i'll get back to you on that

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionGodMode » Tue Oct 28, 2014 8:16 pm At first i didn't expect a mind fuck experience from Eva, so pretty much i watch it at first for entertainment value. I was not disappointed, the battles were beautifully animated, the Evas were cool looking and the characters all felt realistic and believable so that made it that much better to watch. The angels were all defeated in different manners, so that kept the fights from getting boring. The entertainment value of eva lies in the first 19 episodes, not to say 20-24 weren't entertaining, but they were more psychological experiences meant to mess with viewers head than to entertain them.

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Thu Oct 30, 2014 4:02 pm Seeing Rei naked was pretty entertaining.

[]:             Quote Post by strawhatpatriot » Fri Oct 31, 2014 8:46 am The way the series focused on the psychological crap during the last 9 episodes. IT was entertaining seeing how depressive the episodes were. I will never forget hearing "Rei I" and "Thanatos" being used a lot at that time.

[]:             Quote Post by SawItAtAge10 » Mon Nov 03, 2014 5:39 pm The things I found entertaining about the original series were the epic fights, especially the action arc (the fight in episode 19 being the most brutal Angel (physical) attack imo). I also like the characters' "off days" when they were just sitting around having conversations or were at school etc. These held some pretty humorous and humanizing moments that were fun to watch.
summary:
[]: Entertainment Value of Evangelion             Quote Post by Tumbling Down » Sat Oct 25, 2014 11:02 pm What is it you find entertaining about Evangelion? Not intellectually interesting, but entertaining .

[]:             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Sat Oct 25, 2014 11:54 pm Meh, entertainment value for NGE isn't very high, Rebuild on the other hand is chock full of entertain
